---
title: "Start"
subtitle: "Five short pages, about 30 minutes. By the end you can load, inspect, analyze, and save a dataset end-to-end."
sidebar: start
---

This is the practical on-ramp. Each page does one real task and shows the result, then explains — briefly — why the API is shaped the way it is.

| # | Page | What you'll do |
|---|------|----------------|
| 01 | [Read an image](01-image.qmd) | Load a NIfTI file and get your data, with its spatial frame attached. |
| 02 | [Spatial frames](02-space.qmd) | Check that two images line up before you combine them. |
| 03 | [ROI extraction](03-roi.qmd) | Pull a region's time-by-voxel matrix and keep track of where it came from. |
| 04 | [Derived maps](04-derived-maps.qmd) | Compute a map (e.g. temporal SNR) that remembers how it was made. |
| 05 | [Round-trip](05-roundtrip.qmd) | Save it and read it back — provenance still intact, no sidecar files. |

Each page ends with a short, skippable **What it refuses** note: the few inputs neuroim rejects on purpose so a mistake fails loudly instead of producing wrong numbers. Read it if you want the edges; skip it on a first pass.

Prefer vocabulary first? The [Concepts](../concepts/space.qmd) pages are short and indexed.
